topStories.title: Trump meets Japan PM Sanae Takaichi at White House amid US-Iran war, Hormuz Strait crisis: What's on agenda?
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump welcomed Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to the White House on Thursday for a summit. Takaichi's visit comes amid tensions in the Middle East after the US and Israel launched a war with Iran. Here's what's expected from the meeting.

topStories.title: Chhattisgarh Assembly passes new anti-conversion Bill with stricter provisions
topStories.description: The Bill mandates life imprisonment and fines up to ₹25 lakh in specific cases; Congress MLAs raise objections, call for broader consultations, and seek the handing over of the Bill to a ‘Select Committee’

topStories.title: Israeli officials contradict Trump, say US knew of Iran gas strike
topStories.description: Israeli officials contradict Trump say US knew of Iran gas strike

topStories.title: Hegseth drops blunt line: ‘It takes money to kill bad guys’ amid Pentagon seeking $200 billion more for Iran war effort
topStories.description: As the Trump administration weighs its military and diplomatic options, lawmakers are making clear that any funding approval will require detailed justification, strategic clarity, and sustained engagement with Congress.

topStories.title: Nine-judge Supreme Court Bench reserves verdict on ambit of ‘industry’
topStories.description: The 1978 judgment expanded the ambit of ‘industry’ to hold that any systematic activity including charitable and government-run services, were an industry if they met the ‘triple test’ parameters

topStories.title: He served 5 years as MLA… then was asked to repay ₹21 lakh
topStories.description: In our previous episode, we explored the remarkable story of a father and son — P. H. Pandian and Manoj Pandian — who lost elections but successfully challenged the election of the winning candidtes in the Supreme Court. In this epside, we continue that story.

topStories.description: If confirmed, the episode would mark the first instance of Iran striking a US aircraft since the conflict began in February. The F-35, one of the most advanced fighter jets in service, carries a price...

topStories.description: It comes days after Trump criticised Japan for not answering his demands for help in the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.description: Among the 22 names in Thursday’s list was sitting legislator Asif Mohamad Nazar, who has been again fielded from the Laharighat seat.

dailyNews.description: The sharp exchange comes amid heightened tensions following Israeli airstrikes on Iran's South Pars gas field-the world's largest natural gas reserve.

dailyNews.title: US-Iran war: Pilots' body urges flight crews to stay vigilant in West Asia air spaces, flags ‘increased risk’
dailyNews.description: The association recommended that pilots thoroughly evaluate all mission briefings, NOTAMS, and internal corporate guidelines before departure.

dailyNews.description: During his confirmation hearing, Mullin faced criticism over past remarks and immigration policies but signaled a softer stance than former DHS chief Kristi Noem, including requiring warrants for home entries.

dailyNews.description: During the discussion on the bill, Sharma, who also holds Home portfolio, accused the Congress of indulging in vote bank politics.

dailyNews.description: Continuing with her tirade against the Election Commission, the Trinamool Congress supremo claimed that such action amounted to "systematic politicisation of institutions" and a "direct assault on the Constitution"

dailyNews.title: Middle East crisis: Iran signals willingness to prolong conflict despite losing top leaders, demands new ‘status quo’
dailyNews.description: The US and Israeli military strikes have caused devastating losses for Iran, with several of its top leaders killed. However, the surviving leaders continue to project an escalatory rhetoric.

dailyNews.description: The UK's two military bases on Cyprus are a "colonial consequence" on the island, says President Nikos Christodoulides.

dailyNews.title: Attending the Super Bowl is bucket-list experience that is unaffordable for most Americans
dailyNews.description: Attending the Super Bowl in person is a bucket-list item that will never get checked off for many Americans as it is simply too costly for almost any NFL fan.

dailyNews.description: The request, which the White House has not submitted to Congress, is already encountering some resistance.

dailyNews.title: ‘Go Big and Go Loud’: Inside the Justice Dept.’s Push to Prosecute Protesters
dailyNews.description: Prosecutors have struggled to prove in court what the president and his aides have repeatedly said in public: that a network of leftist activists presents a serious threat to national security.

dailyNews.title: Lawsuit Accuses Justice Dept. Leadership of ‘Political Retribution’
dailyNews.description: The suit filed by two fired F.B.I. agents aimed to hold the Trump administration accountable for the purge of personnel who had worked on the investigations of Mr. Trump or his allies.

dailyNews.title: Trump Vowed to Crack Down on Fraudsters, but He’s Pardoned Dozens
dailyNews.description: Across both of his terms, President Trump has granted clemency to more than 70 allies, donors and others convicted in fraud cases.

dailyNews.title: A Gift From Trump to the Supreme Court
dailyNews.description: In a caustic critique of the court issued on social media late Sunday night, the president inadvertently buttressed its independence.

dailyNews.title: Iran War Narrows Fed’s Path to Rate Cuts as Trump Demands Immediate Relief
dailyNews.description: Jerome H. Powell, the Fed chair, wants to keep the central bank’s policy options open as officials stare down another economic shock that threatens to stoke inflation and crimp growth.

dailyNews.title: Ex-FBI agents who worked on Trump 2020 election probe sue over firings
dailyNews.description: Two former FBI agents who helped investigate President Trump's efforts to overturn the 2020 presidential election results sued the federal government, alleging they were wrongfully terminated.

dailyNews.title: Trump meets with Japanese prime minister after urging nation to help police Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: President Trump met Thursday with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi at the White House as he continued his push for U.S. allies to help secure the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran war. CBS News White House reporter Olivia Rinaldi has more.

dailyNews.title: Trump says "I'm not putting troops" in Iran during meeting with Japan's prime minister
dailyNews.description: President Trump took questions from the Oval Office during a meeting with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and said he was not thinking of putting troops in Iran. CBS News' Eleanor Watson breaks down Mr. Trump's latest comments.

dailyNews.title: Trump brings up Pearl Harbor while meeting with Japanese prime minister
dailyNews.description: President Trump met with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi in the Oval Office Thursday and invoked Pearl Harbor when a reporter asked why he didn't give U.S. allies advanced notice before the initial strikes against Iran. CBS News White House reporter Olivia Rinaldi has more.

dailyNews.title: Trump claims the U.S. did not know Israel was attacking the South Pars gas field in Iran
dailyNews.description: President Trump is trying to distance the U.S. from an Israeli strike against Iran's South Pars gas field. CBS News' Courtney Kealy explains.

dailyNews.title: Hegseth says potential $200 billion Iran war spending request could shift: 'Takes money to kill bad guys'
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump's National Economic Council director, Kevin Hassett, said Sunday that the war has already cost $12 billion.
